decks.notes: The main theme of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's plays while maintaining card advantage through counterspells, removal, and card draw. The deck focuses on using spells like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Spell Snare to keep the opponent's threats in check, while also utilizing cards like Snapcaster Mage and Cling to Dust to recycle key cards. Additionally, cards like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian provide additional card advantage, while Field of Ruin serves as land destruction. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and dictate the pace of the game.

This deck aims to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy by combining creatures with abilities that trigger upon entering the battlefield, such as Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, with cards that allow for repeated blinks or flickers like Ephemerate and Consign to Memory. The deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with consistent pressure and disruption, while also having access to removal and control elements like Solitude and Teferi, Time Raveler. The mana base is optimized for consistency and the inclusion of unique spells like Quantum Riddler adds an element of unpredictability to the deck.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures and using a mix of burn spells and combat tricks to control the battlefield. With a mix of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and close out the game before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provide additional reach and removal options to ensure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bring them back onto the battlefield using reanimation spells like Persist and Unearth. This combo strategy allows the player to cheat expensive creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones into play early in the game, overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats. Thoughtseize and Spell Pierce help disrupt the opponent's strategy while Faithless Looting and Psychic Frog help dig for combo pieces. The deck aims to win by overwhelming the opponent with high impact creatures and controlling the game tempo.

The main concept of this deck is to efficiently ramp up mana with cards like Arboreal Grazer and Amulet of Vigor, while utilizing lands like Simic Growth Chamber and Lotus Field for extra value. The goal is to quickly bring out Primeval Titan and search for key lands like Boseiju, Who Endures or Urza's Saga to support your combo win condition. Cards like Scapeshift and Summoner's Pact are essential for finding and securing the necessary pieces for the combo, allowing you to overwhelm your opponent with powerful plays and land triggers.

This deck focuses on aggressive play by utilizing creatures with powerful abilities and utilizing blink effects to gain value. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phage, Titan of Fire's Fury, while also utilizing cards like Arena of Glory and Teferi, Time Raveler to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes cards like Consign to Memory and Ephemerate to provide additional value by blinking creatures and gaining card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to be fast and efficient in putting pressure on the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with cards like Faithless Looting and Burning Inquiry to bring out powerful creatures like Hollow One and Detective's Phoenix. Aggressive creatures like Marauding Mako and Nethergoyf are used to apply pressure on the opponent while spells like Lightning Bolt and Seal of Fire provide additional removal and reach. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of early aggression, graveyard recursion, and burn spells to secure a quick victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game without relying on traditional lands. By utilizing cards like Lotus Bloom, Whir of Invention, and Hydroelectric Specimen, the player can effectively cast spells and disrupt their opponent's strategy. The goal is to use cards like Goblin Charbelcher and Fallaji Archaeologist to deal massive damage and control the board with counterspells like Flare of Denial and Force of Negation. Overall, this deck focuses on a landless combo strategy to outmaneuver traditional land-based decks in Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this deck is to use a combo strategy that does not rely on lands, instead utilizing a variety of creatures and spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up for a game-winning combo with Goblin Charbelcher. The deck also includes cards for card draw, control elements, and protection. Overall, this deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent before they can react effectively.

This deck focuses on quickly bringing out powerful creatures like Hollow One and Detective's Phoenix by utilizing cards that allow for quick and aggressive plays, such as Burning Inquiry and Faithless Looting. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with high damage output and outpace their own strategies, while controlling the board with removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Fatal Push. The deck aims to win the game through fast, aggressive plays and a mix of discard, card draw, and removal.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures and cards that provide temporary removal or protection to maintain board control. By continuously blinking or bouncing key threats and generating card advantage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and disruptive playstyle. The inclusion of cards like Consign to Memory, Teferi, Time Raveler, and Solitude allows for flexibility in dealing with threats and maintaining tempo. The deck also includes a strong mana base to ensure consistency in casting spells and maximizing efficiency.

This deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures and powerful blink effects to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By constantly blinking creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, the deck can generate immense value and tempo advantage. Consign to Memory and Ephemerate provide additional ways to utilize blink effects, while cards like Quantum Riddler and Casey Jones, Vigilante offer disruption and additional aggression. The goal is to outpace the opponent and overwhelm them with a combination of aggressive creatures and powerful blink effects.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly and aggressively deploying creatures and using burn spells to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck includes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and versatile creatures like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to apply pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge allows for additional reach and flexibility in dealing with threats. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of fast creatures and efficient removal spells.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, while also utilizing burn spells such as Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board. The deck includes ways to generate tokens and pump up its creatures with cards like Ocelot Pride and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. The inclusion of Seasoned Pyromancer provides card advantage and helps maintain pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to close out games quickly through a combination of fast creatures and burn spells.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures that can also benefit from blink effects to gain additional value. By utilizing cards like Quantum Riddler and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, the deck aims to put pressure on opponents quickly while also being able to protect its creatures with spells like Solitude and Ephemerate. Consign to Memory can provide card advantage and tempo plays, while Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury can be a strong finisher. The deck also includes removal spells like Prismatic Ending to deal with threats, creating a versatile and resilient strategy.

The deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with blink effects to generate value and put pressure on the opponent. The deck includes powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, along with spells like Consign to Memory and Quantum Riddler to control the board. The blink effects provided by cards like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler allow the player to reuse enter-the-battlefield triggers and protect their creatures. Overall, the key concept of the deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells while maintaining board control through blink effects.
